name: CCode Rust Agent
description: Wire existing 10 Rust crates into a fully-featured AI coding agent CLI per all 15 spec docs, from core UX polish through MCP, KAIROS, Bridge, ULTRAPLAN, TEAMMEM, and Multi-Agent orchestration.

userStories.title: Workspace structure validation and dependency direction enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Cargo workspace structure and crate dependency directions validated so that architectural invariants hold from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: Workspace contains all 13 crates listed in README.md §2 (domain, ports, config, provider, tools, mcp-runtime, remote-runtime, platform, application, session, cron, cli, state-store)
Each new crate (mcp-runtime, remote-runtime, platform) added as workspace member in root Cargo.toml
Dependency direction enforced: domain has zero internal deps; ports depends only on domain; application depends on ports+domain; cli depends on application+bootstrap only
Forbidden edges verified: domain must NOT depend on ports/provider/tools/cli; ports must NOT depend on provider/tools/cli
CI script or test validates dependency direction via `cargo metadata` DAG check
README.md §4 dependency rules encoded as automated check
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per README.md §2 and §4. Create a workspace_lint test or script that parses `cargo metadata --format-version 1` and asserts no forbidden edges exist.

userStories.title: REPL multi-turn conversation mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run `ccode repl` and have a persistent multi-turn conversation so that I can iterate on tasks without restarting.
acceptanceCriteria: `ccode repl` starts an interactive prompt loop
Each input round uses the same session, preserving full conversation history
Supports `/exit` or Ctrl-D to quit
Supports `/clear` to reset session history
Session auto-persists to file via FileSessionRepo
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Implement in crates/cli/src/cmd/repl.rs. Use bootstrap::wire_from_config_with_cwd() to get AppState, then loop stdin → AgentRunCommand::run() with same session_id.

userStories.title: Streaming output formatting
userStories.description: As a user, I want streaming output to display cleanly with proper formatting so that the experience feels polished.
acceptanceCriteria: LLM delta tokens print incrementally without extra newlines or garbled text
Tool call start displays tool name and parameter summary
Tool result displays completion status (success/failure)
Error messages show clear category (auth / network / rate limit)
Markdown code blocks display correctly in terminal
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Touch crates/cli/src/cmd/agent.rs streaming delta handler. Consider a small render helper for markdown formatting.

userStories.title: Tool confirmation UX optimization
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear tool approval UX so that I can quickly allow or deny tool executions.
acceptanceCriteria: Before tool execution, display: tool name, parameters, risk level
Support `y` (allow), `n` (deny), `a` (always allow this tool in session)
Always-allowed tools skip confirmation for the rest of the session
Denial continues the agent loop without aborting the conversation
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Existing agent.rs has basic confirmation. Enhance the prompt formatting and add 'a' (always) option with a HashSet<String> tracker.

userStories.title: Session management CLI commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list, view, and delete sessions so that I can manage my conversation history.
acceptanceCriteria: `ccode sessions list` shows session summaries (ID, timestamp, message count)
`ccode sessions show <id>` displays full conversation content
`ccode sessions delete <id>` removes the specified session
`ccode sessions clear` deletes all sessions (with confirmation prompt)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Implement in crates/cli/src/cmd/sessions.rs. Use SessionRepository trait from ports.

userStories.title: Cron management CLI commands
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage cron jobs via CLI so that I can schedule recurring agent tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: `ccode cron list` displays all scheduled tasks
`ccode cron create --schedule '...' --message '...'` creates a task
`ccode cron delete <id>` removes a task
`ccode cron run <id>` manually triggers one execution
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Implement in crates/cli/src/cmd/cron.rs. Use CronRepository from ports and FileCronRepo from cron crate.

userStories.title: Unified error handling and user messages
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent error messages across all commands so that I can understand what went wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: All crate error types convert to user-friendly messages at CLI layer
Network errors suggest checking connectivity
Auth errors suggest checking API key configuration
Missing config suggests config.example.toml location
Unexpected errors include correlation context (session id, provider name)
Unified error envelope with categories: policy / auth / transport / state / validation per integration-contracts.md
Each error carries a correlation ID for traceability
Remote endpoint errors do not leak internal details
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add a display_error() helper in cli crate that pattern-matches on AppError/WireError/ConfigError variants and prints user-friendly output. Error envelope from integration-contracts.md.

userStories.title: Canonical LLM types in domain crate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want canonical LLM types (ContentBlock, LlmRequest, LlmResponse, StreamEvent) defined in domain so that all crates share one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: ContentBlock enum with variants: Text { text }, ToolUse { id, name, input }, ToolResult { tool_use_id, content, is_error }, Thinking { thinking }
LlmRequest struct with: model, messages (Vec<Message>), tools (Vec<ToolDefinition>), max_tokens, system prompt, stop_sequences, temperature
LlmResponse struct with: content (Vec<ContentBlock>), stop_reason (StopReason), usage (TokenUsage), model
StopReason enum: EndTurn, ToolUse, MaxTokens, StopSequence
StreamEvent enum: ContentDelta { index, text }, ToolCallDelta { index, id, name, input_json }, MessageStart, MessageComplete { stop_reason, usage }
TokenUsage struct: input_tokens (u32), output_tokens (u32)
ToolDefinition struct with: name, description, input_schema (serde_json::Value)
Role enum: User, Assistant, System
Message struct with: role (Role), content (Vec<ContentBlock>)
All types derive Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, Clone
Constants classified per llm-compat-contract.md §8: api-types (enum variants, tag names), config (model alias table, env var names), core-domain (role/stop invariants)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add new module crates/domain/src/llm.rs. These are the Canonical types from llm-compat-contract.md Section 2. Include Thinking variant for extended thinking support.

userStories.title: LlmClient trait and LlmError taxonomy
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single LlmClient trait with comprehensive error types replacing the current ProviderPort so that the interface matches llm-compat-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: LlmClient trait with: complete(&self, req: LlmRequest) -> Result<LlmResponse, LlmError>
LlmClient trait with: stream(&self, req: LlmRequest) -> Result<Pin<Box<dyn Stream<Item = Result<StreamEvent, LlmError>> + Send>>, LlmError>
LlmError enum variant: AuthError(String) — authentication failed
LlmError enum variant: RateLimited { retry_after_ms: Option<u64> } — rate limited by provider
LlmError enum variant: ModelNotAvailable(String) — model not found or unavailable
LlmError enum variant: RequestTooLarge(String) — request exceeds size limits
LlmError enum variant: InvalidResponse(String) — provider returned invalid response body
LlmError enum variant: StreamInterrupted(String) — streaming was interrupted
LlmError enum variant: Network(String) — network/connectivity error
LlmError enum variant: Timeout(Duration) — operation timed out
LlmError enum variant: ProviderError { status: u16, message: String } — generic provider error with HTTP status
LlmClient defined in ports crate, replaces ProviderPort
All downstream consumers (application, tools, bootstrap) updated to use LlmClient
app-services never sees raw HTTP status codes or provider error formats (policy rule)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Rename/refactor ProviderPort → LlmClient in crates/ports/src/provider.rs. Full error taxonomy from llm-compat-contract.md §4. Stream return type uses Pin<Box<dyn Stream>>.

userStories.title: Provider adapter triple conversion
userStories.description: As a developer, I want provider adapters to convert between Canonical and wire formats so that no provider-specific types leak above the provider crate.
acceptanceCriteria: AnthropicAdapter converts Canonical LlmRequest → Anthropic Messages API request format
AnthropicAdapter converts Anthropic Messages API response → Canonical LlmResponse
OpenAiCompatAdapter converts Canonical LlmRequest → OpenAI Chat Completions request format
OpenAiCompatAdapter converts OpenAI Chat Completions response → Canonical LlmResponse
ToolDefinition bidirectional conversion: Canonical schema ↔ Anthropic tool schema
ToolDefinition bidirectional conversion: Canonical schema ↔ OpenAI function schema
StopReason::ToolUse maps correctly to/from both provider representations
Streaming: Anthropic SSE events convert to Canonical StreamEvent sequence with ordering preserved
Streaming: OpenAI SSE events convert to Canonical StreamEvent sequence with ordering preserved
HTTP error mapping inside adapters: 401/403 → LlmError::AuthError
HTTP error mapping inside adapters: 429 → LlmError::RateLimited with retry-after extraction
HTTP error mapping inside adapters: 413/400 (too large) → LlmError::RequestTooLarge
HTTP error mapping inside adapters: network timeout → LlmError::Timeout
HTTP error mapping inside adapters: malformed response body → LlmError::InvalidResponse
All adapters implement the new LlmClient trait
Router implements LlmClient (failover/round-robin logic preserved)
Upper crates (application, tools) only use Canonical types — no provider wire types leak above provider crate
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Refactor crates/provider/src/compat/, openai_compat/, anthropic_compat/ to use Canonical types from domain. Provider wire format field names, SSE event type strings, HTTP header constants are runtime-local constants owned by provider crate only.

userStories.title: MCP runtime crate — transport layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new mcp-runtime crate that handles MCP protocol transport so that the agent can connect to external MCP servers.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/mcp-runtime added as workspace member
Stdio transport implemented (spawn child process, stdin/stdout JSON-RPC)
McpTransport trait with: send(), receive(), close()
TransportError type for connection/protocol failures
Connection failure returns clear error classification
Unit tests for message serialization/deserialization
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Create crates/mcp-runtime/. Implement stdio transport first per chicago-mcp-contract.md. SSE transport can come later.

userStories.title: MCP tool discovery and registration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the agent to discover tools from MCP servers and make them available for use.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup, read MCP server list from config
Call tools/list on each server to get tool definitions
Convert MCP tools to ToolPort interface and register in ToolRegistry
MCP tool execution forwards via tools/call to the corresponding server
Server disconnect auto-removes its tools from registry
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add MCP config section to crates/config. Wire discovery in bootstrap. Create McpToolAdapter implementing ToolPort.

userStories.title: MCP reserved name, capability policy, and lifecycle hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reserved MCP server names, capability checks, and lifecycle hooks enforced per chicago-mcp-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: McpCapabilityPolicy trait with: validate_server_name(&self, name) -> Result<(), McpPolicyError> and capability_level(&self, server) -> CapabilityLevel
CapabilityLevel enum: Standard, PrivilegedComputerUse
Reserved server names (e.g. 'computer') rejected with ReservedServerName error
Privileged computer-use adapter requires feature gate + policy pass to enable
ComputerUseLifecycle trait with: before_tool_call(), after_turn_cleanup(), on_interrupt_cleanup()
Cleanup must run in both normal and interrupt exit paths
Cleanup failures are observable (logged) but do not crash the main turn path
Capability negotiation: only enable capabilities the server declares support for
McpPolicyError variants: ReservedServerName, FeatureGateDisabled, PrivilegedCapabilityDenied, TransportError, InvalidToolPayload, CleanupFailed
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add reserved name list and policy check in mcp-runtime. Wire cleanup hook into agent loop in application crate. ComputerUseLifecycle per chicago-mcp-contract.md.

userStories.title: KAIROS AssistantModeService implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want KAIROS mode resolution and prompt composition so that the agent can operate in different assistant modes.
acceptanceCriteria: AssistantModeService trait with: resolve_mode(ctx: AssistantModeContext) -> AssistantModeDecision
AssistantModeService trait with: build_prompt(ctx: PromptComposeContext) -> PromptComposeResult
AssistantModeService trait with: route_input(ctx: RouteInputContext) -> RouteDecision
resolve_mode(): determines effective mode from config + session state; result is deterministic
build_prompt(): composes system prompt by deterministic precedence order
route_input(): slash command routing (e.g. /brief switches mode); precedence is deterministic
Mode switch emits a DomainEvent with stable metadata
Tool visibility is a pure function of mode + capability policy (no side effects)
Telemetry tag emission uses schema-only fields (no payload leakage)
KairosError variants: DisabledByPolicy, InvalidModeState, PromptComposeFailed(String), RouteConflict
Unit tests for mode resolution logic
Unit tests for prompt precedence ordering
Unit tests for route conflict handling
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per kairos-contract.md. Add mode types to domain, service to application. Prompt precedence must be deterministic and testable.

userStories.title: Mode-aware tool visibility
userStories.description: As a user, I want tool visibility to change based on the current assistant mode so that irrelevant tools are hidden.
acceptanceCriteria: Each mode defines a tool allowlist
ToolRegistry supports filtered view by mode via definitions_for_mode(mode) method
Brief mode hides high-risk tools
Tool allowlist output is deterministic and testable
Mode switch takes effect immediately without restart
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Add definitions_for_mode(mode) method to ToolRegistry. Wire into agent loop's tool list construction.

userStories.title: ModeCoordinatorService implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want mode coordination logic per coordinator-mode-contract.md so that env mode and session mode are reconciled deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: ModeCoordinatorService trait with: resolve_effective_mode(input: ModeResolutionInput) -> EffectiveMode
ModeCoordinatorService trait with: reconcile_on_resume(session_mode: Option<SessionMode>) -> ModeReconcileResult
Explicit precedence rule: session mode takes precedence over env mode (CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE)
reconcile_on_resume(): handles mode conflict when resuming a session deterministically
Mode switch event carries stable metadata (from, to, reason)
CoordinatorModeError variants: DisabledByPolicy, InvalidModeTransition, SessionModeMismatch
Invalid transitions return InvalidModeTransition error
Unit tests for precedence logic
Unit tests for reconciliation on resume
Unit tests for each error variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per coordinator-mode-contract.md. Implement in application crate. Env var: CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE.

userStories.title: TriggerSchedulerService local scheduling
userStories.description: As a user, I want to schedule durable agent tasks that survive process restart so that I can automate recurring workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: TriggerSchedulerService trait with: create(task: TriggerTask) -> Result<TriggerTask, TriggerError>
TriggerSchedulerService trait with: list() -> Result<Vec<TriggerTask>, TriggerError>
TriggerSchedulerService trait with: delete(id, actor: TriggerOwner) -> Result<(), TriggerError>
TriggerTask supports SessionOnly and Durable scope
Durable tasks persist to state-store and reload on restart
SessionOnly tasks survive runtime tick but not process restart
TriggerOwner model: MainAgent, Teammate(String), TeamLead
Durable tasks require explicit user intent
Teammate cannot create Durable tasks (returns DurableNotAllowedForTeammate)
Delete checks owner identity before removing (returns OwnershipViolation on mismatch)
TriggerError variants: GateDisabled, InvalidCron, OwnershipViolation, DurableNotAllowedForTeammate, Unauthorized, UpstreamRemoteError, StorageError
Unit tests for ownership rules
Unit tests for scope rules (SessionOnly vs Durable)
Unit tests for each error variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per agent-triggers-contract.md. Add trigger types to domain, service to application, storage to session/cron crate.

userStories.title: RemoteTriggerDispatchService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want remote trigger dispatch so that scheduled tasks can execute on remote infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: RemoteTriggerDispatchService trait with: dispatch(payload: serde_json::Value) -> Result<String, TriggerError>
Feature gate controls availability (gate off returns GateDisabled)
Requires auth/org context validation (missing auth returns Unauthorized)
Transport errors mapped to UpstreamRemoteError (do not leak internal details)
Local scheduling works independently when remote is disabled
Unit tests for gate-off path
Unit tests for auth validation
Unit tests for error non-leakage
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per agent-triggers-contract.md. Depends on remote-runtime crate existing. Wire feature gate in config.

userStories.title: Remote runtime crate — RemoteSessionService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want bridge mode remote session management per bridge-mode-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/remote-runtime added as workspace member
RemoteSessionService trait with: create_session(req) -> Result<RemoteSessionSummary, RemoteSessionError>
RemoteSessionService trait with: fetch_session(session_id) -> Result<RemoteSessionSummary, RemoteSessionError>
RemoteSessionService trait with: archive_session(session_id, policy) -> Result<(), RemoteSessionError>
RemoteSessionService trait with: update_title(session_id, title) -> Result<(), RemoteSessionError>
RemoteSessionService trait with: reconcile_resume(session_id) -> Result<RemoteSessionSummary, RemoteSessionError>
RemoteSessionState state machine with 7 states: Pending, Running, Idle, RequiresAction, Archived, Expired, Failed
Allowed transitions: Pending→Running|Idle|RequiresAction|Failed; Running→Idle|RequiresAction|Archived|Failed|Expired; Idle→Running|Archived|Expired; RequiresAction→Running|Archived|Expired
Terminal states: Archived, Expired, Failed (no outgoing transitions)
Forbidden transitions enforced: Archived→Running, Expired→Running
Archive is idempotent (409 treated as success-equivalent)
RemoteSessionError variants: DisabledByPolicy, EntitlementDenied, InvalidStateTransition, NotFound, Expired, AuthUnavailable, Upstream(String)
Missing token/org context is a policy error in app-services; runtime returns typed transport/auth errors only
Shutdown path can degrade to best-effort archive under timeout budget
Runtime errors translated in app-services into stable RemoteSessionError
Unit tests for all state transitions (allowed + forbidden)
Unit tests for 409 idempotent archive
Unit tests for each error variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per bridge-mode-contract.md. Create crates/remote-runtime/. State types go in domain, service trait in ports, impl in application.

userStories.title: CcrClient HTTP transport layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an HTTP client for remote session API communication.
acceptanceCriteria: CcrClient trait with: create(req) -> Result<RemoteSessionSummary, CcrClientError>
CcrClient trait with: get(session_id) -> Result<RemoteSessionSummary, CcrClientError>
CcrClient trait with: archive(session_id) -> Result<ArchiveResult, CcrClientError>
CcrClient trait with: patch_title(session_id, title) -> Result<(), CcrClientError>
Session ID prefix translation (session_ vs cse_) handled only in remote-runtime — app-services never manipulates tag prefixes directly
Auth token and org context auto-attached to requests
Timeout and retry configurable via config
CcrClientError variants: Http(String), Timeout, Unauthorized, Forbidden, InvalidPayload
CcrClientError and RemoteSessionError are separate error layers with translation in app-services
Prefix translation (session_ vs cse_) round-trips correctly through compatibility adapter
Unit tests for prefix translation round-trip
Unit tests for each CcrClientError variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per bridge-mode-contract.md. Implement in crates/remote-runtime/src/client.rs. Use reqwest. Prefix translation is transport compatibility logic, not domain logic.

userStories.title: UltraplanService state machine
userStories.description: As a user, I want to launch long-running planning sessions on remote infrastructure with lifecycle management.
acceptanceCriteria: UltraplanService trait with: launch(prompt, policy) -> Result<UltraplanSession, UltraplanError>
UltraplanService trait with: poll(session_id) -> Result<UltraplanPhase, UltraplanError>
UltraplanService trait with: stop(session_id) -> Result<(), UltraplanError>
UltraplanService trait with: archive_orphan(session_id) -> Result<(), UltraplanError>
8-state machine (UltraplanPhase): Idle, Launching, Polling, AwaitingInput, Approved, Stopping, Completed, Failed
Transitions: Idle→Launching; Launching→Polling|Failed; Polling→AwaitingInput|Approved|Failed|Stopping; AwaitingInput→Polling|Stopping; Approved→Completed; Stopping→Completed|Failed
Terminal states: Completed, Failed
At most one active ULTRAPLAN per session context
Launch while already Launching/Polling returns AlreadyActive
Keyword auto-route must respect launch/polling guards
Failure after remote session creation auto-attempts orphan archive
Stop clears local active marker before returning
UltraplanError variants: DisabledByPolicy, AlreadyActive, LaunchFailed, PollTimeout, ApprovalFailed, ArchiveFailed, Transport(String)
Unit tests for all state transitions
Unit tests for guard conditions (AlreadyActive, concurrent launch)
Unit tests for orphan archive on failure path
Unit tests for each error variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per ultraplan-contract.md. Implement in application crate, using remote-runtime for transport.

userStories.title: Platform crate — secure path validation and secret scanning
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a platform crate for secure path validation and filesystem abstractions needed by TEAMMEM.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/platform added as workspace member (or integrated into existing crate)
validate_canonical_path(): rejects '..', symlink escape, paths outside root
SecretScanner: scans file content for secret patterns
Scan results include rule_id and label for auditing
All local writes pass canonical path validation first (policy rule)
All remote writes pass secret scan first (policy rule)
Invariant ordering enforced: validate → scan → write/push
Unit tests for path traversal attack scenarios
Unit tests for symlink escape scenarios
Unit tests for secret pattern detection
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per teammem-contract.md. This is a prerequisite for TEAMMEM sync. Invariant ordering: validate → scan → write/push is critical.

userStories.title: TeamMemorySyncService implementation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sync team memory with remote storage with security guarantees.
acceptanceCriteria: TeamMemorySyncService trait with: pull() -> Result<SyncResult, TeamMemError>
TeamMemorySyncService trait with: push() -> Result<SyncResult, TeamMemError>
TeamMemorySyncService trait with: sync() -> Result<SyncResult, TeamMemError>
Pull → merge → push with ETag / 412 conflict retry (bounded at configured max_retries)
All remote writes pass secret scan first
All local writes pass canonical path validation first
Path traversal / symlink escape returns PathValidationFailed
Secret detected skips file with audit metadata (never returns raw secret content)
File over max-size skipped with explicit classification
Entry count over max-entry has deterministic truncation/skip policy
Retry exhausted returns ConflictExhausted
TeamMemError variants: AuthUnavailable, Unauthorized, ConflictExhausted, PathValidationFailed, SecretDetected, FileTooLarge, EntryLimitExceeded, StorageWriteError, UpstreamTimeout
Unit tests for security invariants (path validation, secret scan)
Unit tests for conflict retry exhaustion
Unit tests for oversized file handling
Unit tests for each error variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per teammem-contract.md. Invariant ordering: validate → scan → write/push. Implement in application crate, using platform + remote-runtime.

userStories.title: MultiAgentOrchestrator implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want parallel agent orchestration per multi-agent-orchestration-contract.md.
acceptanceCriteria: MultiAgentOrchestrator trait with: spawn_parallel(tasks: Vec<WorkerTaskSpec>) -> Result<Vec<String>, OrchestrationError>
MultiAgentOrchestrator trait with: handle_notification(notification: WorkerResultNotification) -> Result<(), OrchestrationError>
MultiAgentOrchestrator trait with: synthesize_summary(task_ids: &[String]) -> Result<CoordinatorSummary, OrchestrationError>
MultiAgentOrchestrator trait with: stop_task(task_id: &str) -> Result<(), OrchestrationError>
WorkerTaskSpec includes criticality: Blocking (blocks main progress) / Sidecar (background, non-blocking)
WorkerStatus enum: Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled
Parallel fan-out rejects overlapping write scopes (PolicyViolation)
Independent tasks must be launched in parallel when no shared write scope exists
Blocking critical-path work should not be delegated if local progress depends on it immediately
Worker prompts are self-contained with ownership boundaries
Coordinator produces synthesized summary from notifications (no raw pass-through)
Notification parsing enforces structured payload fields (task_id, status, summary)
Stop is idempotent — stopping a terminal-state task does not error
OrchestrationError variants: DisabledByMode, InvalidTaskSpec, PolicyViolation, NotificationMalformed, TaskNotFound, SpawnFailed(String), SynthesisFailed(String)
Unit tests for fan-out write-scope conflict policy
Unit tests for synthesis logic
Unit tests for idempotent stop
Unit tests for each error variant
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per multi-agent-orchestration-contract.md. Implement in application crate. Uses tokio::spawn for parallel workers.

userStories.title: AgentTool and TaskStopTool integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the coordinator agent to spawn and manage worker agents via tools.
acceptanceCriteria: AgentTool supports spawning workers with WorkerTaskSpec
TaskStopTool supports stopping a running worker
Worker results reported as WorkerResultNotification
Notification routing to coordinator's handle_notification
Non-coordinator mode blocks orchestration calls with explicit policy error (DisabledByMode)
UI displays worker status (Running / Completed / Failed / Cancelled)
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Extend existing crates/tools/src/spawn_agent.rs. Add TaskStopTool. Wire notification routing in bootstrap.

userStories.title: Cross-feature integration contracts enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cross-feature integration contracts enforced per integration-contracts.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Bridge x Ultraplan: remote session APIs only through app-services
Bridge x Ultraplan: launch failure after session creation always issues archive attempt
Bridge x Ultraplan: archived session cannot re-enter running state
Bridge x Ultraplan: concurrent operations do not corrupt active session pointer state
MCP x Tool Runtime: cleanup hooks run on normal completion and interruption
MCP x Tool Runtime: privileged capability cannot be activated via generic MCP registration
MCP x Tool Runtime: policy failure prevents side effects
TeamMem x KAIROS: secret-detected content is never emitted in prompt payload
TeamMem x KAIROS: invalid path keys are blocked and logged safely
TeamMem x KAIROS: sync conflict retries remain bounded and observable
Triggers x Remote: remote gate-off path keeps local tasks intact
Triggers x Remote: durable/session-only semantics survive restart
Triggers x Remote: ownership violations return deterministic policy errors
Coordinator x Multi-Agent: parallel fan-out occurs only when write-scope conflicts absent
Coordinator x Multi-Agent: mixed worker outcomes produce deterministic aggregate status
Coordinator x Multi-Agent: final summary contains findings and actionable next steps with task traceability
Unified error envelope: policy / auth / transport / state / validation categories with correlation ID
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per integration-contracts.md. Add integration tests asserting each cross-feature boundary rule. Each of the 5 cross-feature pairs has 3 test cases.

userStories.title: Observability baseline
userStories.description: As an operator, I want structured logging and trace IDs across all runtime crates.
acceptanceCriteria: All async operations have tracing spans
Correlation ID propagates from CLI entry to provider call
Error logs include structured context (session_id, provider, tool_name)
RUST_LOG env var controls log level
Sensitive data (API keys, tokens) never appears in logs

userStories.title: Constants classification governance
userStories.description: As a developer, I want constants classified and placed in their owning crate per the constants governance policy defined across all contract docs.
acceptanceCriteria: api-types constants (enum variants, protocol field names, state labels) live in domain crate
config constants (gate keys, timeout defaults, env var names, path defaults, retry caps) live in config crate
core-domain constants (ownership invariants, state transition rules, validated path policies) live in domain crate
runtime-local constants (internal key names, lock file internals, backoff profiles, wire format field names, SSE event type strings, HTTP headers) live in their owning runtime crate only
ui-tui constants (user-facing messages, display labels, help text) live in cli crate
No constant leaks outside its owning crate boundary
Automated test or lint validates constant placement rules
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test --workspace passes
userStories.notes: Per constants classification sections in README.md §6 and all contract docs. Constants categories: api-types, config, core-domain, runtime-local, ui-tui.

Error test: network timeout → LlmError::Timeout
Error test: malformed response body → LlmError::InvalidResponse
Agent loop test: agent loop can alternate between complete() and tool execution using only Canonical types
Agent loop test: switching provider in config requires zero code changes in app-services
